hook:
Cold email is dead.

Or at least, cold email the way most people do it.

2024 deliverability changes killed 80% of campaigns.

Here's what's replacing it:
tweets:
What killed cold email:

1. Google's Feb 2024 update (bulk sender rules)
2. Yahoo's authentication requirements
3. AI detection in spam filters
4. Everyone's inbox is saturated

Open rates dropped from 40% to 8% for most senders.

The people still winning at cold email:

- Warm up domains for 3+ weeks
- Send <50 emails/day per domain
- Use 10+ domains rotating
- Personalize first line with AI that doesn't sound like AI
- Hire deliverability consultants

Expensive. Complicated. Not for beginners.

What's working instead:

1. Cold DMs (X, LinkedIn, Instagram)
2. Loom video outreach
3. Community-based selling
4. Content that attracts inbound
5. Reply-guy strategy

Let's break each down.

Cold DMs on X:

Open rates: 70-80%
Reply rates: 15-25%

Why: People actually check DMs.

The play: Comment on their posts for 2 weeks first. Then DM. "Hey, been following your work on X. Quick question..."

Warm DM > cold DM.

Loom video outreach:

30-second personalized video.

"Hey [name], saw your site [url]. Noticed [specific thing]. Here's one idea..."

Record 20 per day. 4 will reply.

Takes same time as cold email but 5x response rate.

Community-based selling:

Join Slack groups, Discord servers, paid communities.

Be helpful for 30 days. Answer questions.

Then mention what you do when relevant.

No pitch. Just "I actually built something for this exact problem."

$0 CAC. High trust.

Reply-guy strategy:

Find 50 accounts your target customers follow.

Turn on notifications.

Be first to reply with something valuable.

They'll check your profile. Your bio sells for you.

I've gotten 4 clients this way. Zero outreach.

The meta shift:

Old model: Interrupt people with your pitch
New model: Show up where they already are

Inbound > outbound.
Warm > cold.
Few high-quality touches > mass spray.

Cold email isn't fully dead.

But the low-effort version is.

If you're not willing to do it right (proper infrastructure, real personalization, small volume), switch to these alternatives.

They're easier and work better anyway.
cta:
Testing all these methods this quarter.

Follow @PRINTMAXXER for what's actually converting.

What's working for your outreach? Drop it below.
